NKJV.1: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
NKJV.2: As it is written in the Prophets:  “Behold, I send My messenger before Your face, Who will prepare Your way before You.”
NKJV.3: “The voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the LORD; Make His paths straight.’ ”
NKJV.4: John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching a baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
NKJV.5: Then all the land of Judea, and those from Jerusalem, went out to him and were all baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.
NKJV.6: Now John was clothed with camel’s hair and with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
NKJV.7: And he preached, saying, “There comes One after me who is mightier than I, whose sandal strap I am not worthy to stoop down and loose.
NKJV.8: I indeed baptized you with water, but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
NKJV.9: It came to pass in those days that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
NKJV.10: And immediately, coming up from the water, He saw the heavens parting and the Spirit descending upon Him like a dove.
NKJV.11: Then a voice came from heaven, “You are My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”
NKJV.12: Immediately the Spirit drove Him into the wilderness.
NKJV.13: And He was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan, and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered to Him.
NKJV.14: Now after John was put in prison, Jesus came to Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
NKJV.15: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and  the kingdom of God is at hand. Repent, and believe in the gospel.”
NKJV.16: And as He walked by the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
NKJV.17: Then Jesus said to them, “Follow Me, and I will make you become  fishers of men.”
NKJV.18: They immediately left their nets and followed Him.
NKJV.19: When He had gone a little farther from there, He saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the boat mending their nets.
NKJV.20: And immediately He called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, and went after Him.
NKJV.21: Then they went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath He entered the synagogue and taught.
NKJV.22: And they were astonished at His teaching, for He taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
NKJV.23: Now there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit. And he cried out,
NKJV.24: saying, “Let us alone! What have we to do with You, Jesus of Nazareth? Did You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!”
NKJV.25: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him!”
NKJV.26: And when the unclean spirit had convulsed him and cried out with a loud voice, he came out of him.
NKJV.27: Then they were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? What new doctrine is this? For with authority He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him.”
NKJV.28: And immediately His fame spread throughout all the region around Galilee.
NKJV.29: Now as soon as they had come out of the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
NKJV.30: But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick with a fever, and they told Him about her at once.
NKJV.31: So He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up, and immediately the fever left her. And she served them.
NKJV.32: At evening, when the sun had set, they brought to Him all who were sick and those who were demon-possessed.
NKJV.33: And the whole city was gathered together at the door.
NKJV.34: Then He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and He did not allow the demons to speak, because they knew Him.
NKJV.35: Now in the morning, having risen a long while before daylight, He went out and departed to a solitary place; and there He prayed.
NKJV.36: And Simon and those who were with Him searched for Him.
NKJV.37: When they found Him, they said to Him, “Everyone is looking for You.”
NKJV.38: But He said to them, “Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also, because  for this purpose I have come forth.”
NKJV.39: And He was preaching in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and casting out demons.
NKJV.40: Now a leper came to Him, imploring Him, kneeling down to Him and saying to Him, “If You are willing, You can make me clean.”
NKJV.41: Then Jesus, moved with compassion, stretched out His hand and touched him, and said to him, “I am willing; be cleansed.”
NKJV.42: As soon as He had spoken, immediately the leprosy left him, and he was cleansed.
NKJV.43: And He strictly warned him and sent him away at once,
NKJV.44: and said to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go your way, show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing those things  which Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
NKJV.45: However, he went out and began to proclaim it freely, and to spread the matter, so that Jesus could no longer openly enter the city, but was outside in deserted places; and they came to Him from every direction.

ESV.1: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
ESV.2: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet,  “Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way,
ESV.3: the voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare  the way of the Lord, make his paths straight,’”
ESV.4: John appeared, baptizing in the wilderness and proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
ESV.5: And all the country of Judea and all Jerusalem were going out to him and were being baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
ESV.6: Now John was clothed with camel's hair and wore a leather belt around his waist and ate locusts and wild honey.

ESV.8: I have baptized you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
ESV.9: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
ESV.10: And when he came up out of the water, immediately he saw the heavens being torn open and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
ESV.11: And a voice came from heaven, “You are my beloved Son;  with you I am well pleased.”
ESV.12: The Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.
ESV.13: And he was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan. And he was with the wild animals, and the angels were ministering to him.
ESV.14: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee, proclaiming the gospel of God,
ESV.15: and saying,  “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand;  repent and believe in the gospel.”
ESV.16: Passing alongside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew the brother of Simon casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
ESV.17: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you become fishers of men.”
ESV.18: And immediately they left their nets and followed him.
ESV.19: And going on a little farther, he saw James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, who were in their boat mending the nets.
ESV.20: And immediately he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants and followed him.
ESV.21: And they went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath he entered the synagogue and was teaching.
ESV.22: And they were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as one who had authority, and not as the scribes.
ESV.23: And immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit. And he cried out,
ESV.24: “What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God.”
ESV.25: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”
ESV.26: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying out with a loud voice, came out of him.
ESV.27: And they were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
ESV.28: And at once his fame spread everywhere throughout all the surrounding region of Galilee.
ESV.29: And immediately he  left the synagogue and entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
ESV.30: Now Simon's mother-in-law lay ill with a fever, and immediately they told him about her.
ESV.31: And he came and took her by the hand and lifted her up, and the fever left her, and she began to serve them.
ESV.32: That evening at sundown they brought to him all who were sick or oppressed by demons.
ESV.33: And the whole city was gathered together at the door.
ESV.34: And he healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons. And he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.
ESV.35: And rising very early in the morning, while it was still dark, he departed and went out to a desolate place, and there he prayed.
ESV.36: And Simon and those who were with him searched for him,
ESV.37: and they found him and said to him, “Everyone is looking for you.”
ESV.38: And he said to them, “Let us go on to the next towns, that I may preach there also, for that is why I came out.”
ESV.39: And he went throughout all Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and casting out demons.
ESV.40: And a leper  came to him, imploring him, and kneeling said to him, “If you will, you can make me clean.”
ESV.41: Moved with pity, he stretched out his hand and touched him and said to him, “I will; be clean.”
ESV.42: And immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.
ESV.43: And Jesus  sternly charged him and sent him away at once,
ESV.44: and said to him,  “See that you say nothing to anyone, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, for a proof to them.”
ESV.45: But he went out and began to talk freely about it, and to spread the news, so that Jesus could no longer openly enter a town, but was out in desolate places, and people were coming to him from every quarter.

NASB.1: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
NASB.2: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: 'BEHOLD, I SEND MY MESSENGER AHEAD OF YOU, WHO WILL PREPARE YOUR WAY;
NASB.3: THE VOICE OF ONE CRYING IN THE WILDERNESS, 'MAKE READY THE WAY OF THE LORD, MAKE HIS PATHS STRAIGHT.''
NASB.4: John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
NASB.5: And all the country of Judea was going out to him, and all the people of Jerusalem; and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.
NASB.6: John was clothed with camel's hair and [wore] a leather belt around his waist, and his diet was locusts and wild honey.
NASB.7: And he was preaching, and saying, 'After me One is coming who is mightier than I, and I am not fit to stoop down and untie the thong of His sandals.
NASB.8: 'I baptized you with water; but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.'
NASB.9: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
NASB.10: Immediately coming up out of the water, He saw the heavens opening, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him;
NASB.11: and a voice came out of the heavens: 'You are My beloved Son, in You I am well-pleased.'
NASB.12: Immediately the Spirit impelled Him [to] [go] out into the wilderness.
NASB.13: And He was in the wilderness forty days being tempted by Satan; and He was with the wild beasts, and the angels were ministering to Him.
NASB.14: Now after John had been taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of God,
NASB.15: and saying, 'The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe in the gospel.'
NASB.16: As He was going along by the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew, the brother of Simon, casting a net in the sea; for they were fishermen.
NASB.17: And Jesus said to them, 'Follow Me, and I will make you become fishers of men.'
NASB.18: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
NASB.19: Going on a little farther, He saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets.
NASB.20: Immediately He called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, and went away to follow Him.
NASB.21: They went into Capernaum; and immediately on the Sabbath He entered the synagogue and [began] to teach.
NASB.22: They were amazed at His teaching; for He was teaching them as [one] having authority, and not as the scribes.
NASB.23: Just then there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
NASB.24: saying, 'What business do we have with each other, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are-- the Holy One of God!'
NASB.25: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, 'Be quiet, and come out of him!'
NASB.26: Throwing him into convulsions, the unclean spirit cried out with a loud voice and came out of him.
NASB.27: They were all amazed, so that they debated among themselves, saying, 'What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him.'
NASB.28: Immediately the news about Him spread everywhere into all the surrounding district of Galilee.
NASB.29: And immediately after they came out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
NASB.30: Now Simon's mother-in-law was lying sick with a fever; and immediately they spoke to Jesus about her.
NASB.31: And He came to her and raised her up, taking her by the hand, and the fever left her, and she waited on them.
NASB.32: When evening came, after the sun had set, they [began] bringing to Him all who were ill and those who were demon-possessed.
NASB.33: And the whole city had gathered at the door.
NASB.34: And He healed many who were ill with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and He was not permitting the demons to speak, because they knew who He was.
NASB.35: In the early morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left [the house], and went away to a secluded place, and was praying there.
NASB.36: Simon and his companions searched for Him;
NASB.37: they found Him, and said to Him, 'Everyone is looking for You.'
NASB.38: He said to them, 'Let us go somewhere else to the towns nearby, so that I may preach there also; for that is what I came for.'
NASB.39: And He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out the demons.
NASB.40: And a leper came to Jesus, beseeching Him and falling on his knees before Him, and saying, 'If You are willing, You can make me clean.'
NASB.41: Moved with compassion, Jesus stretched out His hand and touched him, and said to him, 'I am willing; be cleansed.'
NASB.42: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cleansed.
NASB.43: And He sternly warned him and immediately sent him away,
NASB.44: and He said to him, 'See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.'
NASB.45: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely and to spread the news around, to such an extent that Jesus could no longer publicly enter a city, but stayed out in unpopulated areas; and they were coming to Him from everywhere.

NLT.1: This is the Good News about Jesus the Messiah, the Son of God. It began
NLT.2:
just as the prophet Isaiah had written: “Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you,
and he will prepare your way.
NLT.3:
He is a voice shouting in the wilderness,
‘Prepare the way for the LORD’s coming!
Clear the road for him!’ ”
NLT.4: This messenger was John the Baptist. He was in the wilderness and preached that people should be baptized to show that they had repented of their sins and turned to God to be forgiven.
NLT.5: All of Judea, including all the people of Jerusalem, went out to see and hear John. And when they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
NLT.6: His clothes were woven from coarse camel hair, and he wore a leather belt around his waist. For food he ate locusts and wild honey.
NLT.7: John announced: “Someone is coming soon who is greater than I am — so much greater that I’m not even worthy to stoop down like a slave and untie the straps of his sandals.
NLT.8: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit!”
NLT.9: One day Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
NLT.10: As Jesus came up out of the water, he saw the heavens splitting apart and the Holy Spirit descending on him like a dove.
NLT.11: And a voice from heaven said, “You are my dearly loved Son, and you bring me great joy.”
NLT.12: The Spirit then compelled Jesus to go into the wilderness,
NLT.13: where he was tempted by Satan for forty days. He was out among the wild animals, and angels took care of him.
NLT.14: Later on, after John was arrested, Jesus went into Galilee, where he preached God’s Good News.
NLT.15: “The time promised by God has come at last!” he announced. “The Kingdom of God is near! Repent of your sins and believe the Good News!”
NLT.16: One day as Jesus was walking along the shore of the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew throwing a net into the water, for they fished for a living.
NLT.17: Jesus called out to them, “Come, follow me, and I will show you how to fish for people!”
NLT.18: And they left their nets at once and followed him.
NLT.19: A little farther up the shore Jesus saw Zebedee’s sons, James and John, in a boat repairing their nets.
NLT.20: He called them at once, and they also followed him, leaving their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired men.
NLT.21: Jesus and his companions went to the town of Capernaum. When the Sabbath day came, he went into the synagogue and began to teach.
NLT.22: The people were amazed at his teaching, for he taught with real authority — quite unlike the teachers of religious law.
NLT.23: Suddenly, a man in the synagogue who was possessed by an evil spirit cried out,
NLT.24: “Why are you interfering with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God!”
NLT.25: But Jesus reprimanded him. “Be quiet! Come out of the man,” he ordered.
NLT.26: At that, the evil spirit screamed, threw the man into a convulsion, and then came out of him.
NLT.27: Amazement gripped the audience, and they began to discuss what had happened. “What sort of new teaching is this?” they asked excitedly. “It has such authority! Even evil spirits obey his orders!”
NLT.28: The news about Jesus spread quickly throughout the entire region of Galilee.
NLT.29: After Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went to Simon and Andrew’s home.
NLT.30: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was sick in bed with a high fever. They told Jesus about her right away.
NLT.31: So he went to her bedside, took her by the hand, and helped her sit up. Then the fever left her, and she prepared a meal for them.
NLT.32: That evening after sunset, many sick and demon-possessed people were brought to Jesus.
NLT.33: The whole town gathered at the door to watch.
NLT.34: So Jesus healed many people who were sick with various diseases, and he cast out many demons. But because the demons knew who he was, he did not allow them to speak.
NLT.35: Before daybreak the next morning, Jesus got up and went out to an isolated place to pray.
NLT.36: Later Simon and the others went out to find him.
NLT.37: When they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you.”
NLT.38: But Jesus replied, “We must go on to other towns as well, and I will preach to them, too. That is why I came.”
NLT.39: So he traveled throughout the region of Galilee, preaching in the synagogues and casting out demons.
NLT.40: A man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus, begging to be healed. “If you are willing, you can heal me and make me clean,” he said.
NLT.41: Moved with compassion, Jesus reached out and touched him. “I am willing,” he said. “Be healed!”
NLT.42: Instantly the leprosy disappeared, and the man was healed.
NLT.43: Then Jesus sent him on his way with a stern warning:
NLT.44: “Don’t tell anyone about this. Instead, go to the priest and let him examine you. Take along the offering required in the law of Moses for those who have been healed of leprosy. This will be a public testimony that you have been cleansed.”
NLT.45: But the man went and spread the word, proclaiming to everyone what had happened. As a result, large crowds soon surrounded Jesus, and he couldn’t publicly enter a town anywhere. He had to stay out in the secluded places, but people from everywhere kept coming to him.

WEB.1: The beginning of the Good News of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

WEB.4: John came baptizing  in the wilderness and preaching the baptism of repentance for forgiveness of sins.

WEB.8: I baptized you in  water, but he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.”
WEB.9: In those days, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

WEB.12: Immediately the Spirit drove him out into the wilderness.
WEB.13: He was there in the wilderness forty days tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals; and the angels were serving him.
WEB.14: Now after John was taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the Good News of God’s Kingdom,
WEB.15: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and God’s Kingdom is at hand! Repent, and believe in the Good News.”
WEB.16: Passing along by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew the brother of Simon casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
WEB.17: Jesus said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you into fishers for men.”
WEB.18: Immediately they left their nets, and followed him.
WEB.19: Going on a little further from there, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John, his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets.
WEB.20: Immediately he called them, and they left their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired servants, and went after him.
WEB.21: They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath day he entered into the synagogue and taught.
WEB.22: They were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as having authority, and not as the scribes.
WEB.23: Immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out,
WEB.24: saying, “Ha! What do we have to do with you, Jesus, you Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”
WEB.25: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him!”
WEB.26: The unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.
WEB.27: They were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching? For with authority he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him!”
WEB.28: The report of him went out immediately everywhere into all the region of Galilee and its surrounding area.
WEB.29: Immediately, when they had come out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
WEB.30: Now Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick with a fever, and immediately they told him about her.
WEB.31: He came and took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
WEB.32: At evening, when the sun had set, they brought to him all who were sick, and those who were possessed by demons.
WEB.33: All the city was gathered together at the door.
WEB.34: He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons. He didn’t allow the demons to speak, because they knew him.
WEB.35: Early in the morning, while it was still dark, he rose up and went out, and departed into a deserted place, and prayed there.
WEB.36: Simon and those who were with him followed after him;
WEB.37: and they found him, and told him, “Everyone is looking for you.”
WEB.38: He said to them, “Let’s go elsewhere into the next towns, that I may preach there also, because I came out for this reason.”
WEB.39: He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out demons.
WEB.40: A leper came to him, begging him, kneeling down to him, and saying to him, “If you want to, you can make me clean.”
WEB.41: Being moved with compassion, he stretched out his hand, and touched him, and said to him, “I want to. Be made clean.”
WEB.42: When he had said this, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was made clean.
WEB.43: He strictly warned him, and immediately sent him out,
WEB.44: and said to him, “See you say nothing to anybody, but go show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing the things which Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”
WEB.45: But he went out, and began to proclaim it much, and to spread about the matter, so that Jesus could no more openly enter into a city, but was outside in desert places: and they came to him from everywhere.

AMP.1: The beginning of the [facts regarding the] good news of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
AMP.2:
As it is written and forever remains in the [writings of the] prophet Isaiah:
"Behold, I send My messenger ahead of You,
Who will prepare Your way—
AMP.3:
A voice of one shouting in the wilderness,
'Prepare the way of the Lord,
Make His paths straight!'"
AMP.4: John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins [that is, requiring a change of one's old way of thinking, turning away from sin and seeking God and His righteousness].
AMP.5: And all the country of Judea and all the people of Jerusalem were continually going out to him; and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, as they confessed their sins.
AMP.6: John wore clothing made of camel's hair and had a [wide] leather band around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
AMP.7: And he was preaching, saying, "After me comes He who is mightier [more powerful, more noble] than I, and I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the straps of His sandals [even as His slave].
AMP.8: As for me, I baptized you [who came to me] with water [only]; but He will baptize you [who truly repent] with the Holy Spirit."
AMP.9: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
AMP.10: Immediately coming up out of the water, he (John) saw the heavens torn open, and the Spirit like a dove descending on Him (Jesus);
AMP.11: and a voice came out of heaven saying: "You are My beloved Son, in You I am well-pleased and delighted!"
AMP.12: Immediately the [Holy] Spirit forced Him out into the wilderness.
AMP.13: He was in the wilderness forty days being tempted [to do evil] by Satan; and He was with the wild animals, and the angels ministered continually to Him.
AMP.14: Now after John [the Baptist] was arrested and taken into custody, Jesus went to Galilee, preaching the good news of [the kingdom of] God,
AMP.15: and saying, "The [appointed period of] time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent [change your inner self—your old way of thinking, regret past sins, live your life in a way that proves repentance; seek God's purpose for your life] and believe [with a deep, abiding trust] in the good news [regarding salvation]."
AMP.16: As Jesus was walking by the shore of the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon [Peter] and Simon's brother, Andrew, casting a net in the sea; for they were fishermen.
AMP.17: And Jesus said to them, " Follow Me [as My disciples, accepting Me as your Master and Teacher and walking the same path of life that I walk], and I will make you fishers of men."
AMP.18: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him [becoming His disciples, believing and trusting in Him and following His example].
AMP.19: Going on a little farther, He saw James the son of Zebedee, and his brother John, who were also in the boat mending and cleaning the nets.
AMP.20: Immediately Jesus called to them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers, and went away to follow Him [becoming His disciples, believing and trusting in Him and following His example].
AMP.21: They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.
AMP.22: They were completely amazed at His teaching; because He was teaching them as one having [God-given] authority, and not as the scribes.
AMP.23: Just then there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit; and he cried out [terribly from the depths of his throat],
AMP.24: saying, " What business do You have with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!"
AMP.25: Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be quiet (muzzled, silenced), and come out of him!"
AMP.26: The unclean spirit threw the man into convulsions, and screeching with a loud voice, came out of him.
AMP.27: They were all so amazed that they debated and questioned each other, saying, "What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits (demons), and they obey Him."
AMP.28: Immediately the news about Him spread everywhere throughout the district surrounding Galilee.
AMP.29: And immediately they left the synagogue and went into the house of Simon [Peter] and Andrew, accompanied by James and John.
AMP.30: Now Simon's mother-in-law was lying sick with a fever; and immediately they told Him about her.
AMP.31: Jesus went to her, and taking her by the hand, raised her up; and the fever left her, and she began to serve them [as her guests].
AMP.32: Now when evening came, after the sun had set [and the Sabbath Day had ended, in a steady stream] they were bringing to Him all who were sick and those who were under the power of demons,
AMP.33: until [it seemed as though] the whole city had gathered together at the door.
AMP.34: And Jesus healed many who were suffering with various diseases; and He drove out many demons, but would not allow the demons to speak, because they knew Him [recognizing Him as the Son of God].
AMP.35: Early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left [the house], and went out to a secluded place, and was praying there.
AMP.36: Simon [Peter] and his companions searched [everywhere, looking anxiously] for Him,
AMP.37: and they found Him and said, "Everybody is looking for You!"
AMP.38: He replied, "Let us go on to the neighboring towns, so I may preach there also; that is why I came [from the Father]."
AMP.39: So He went throughout Galilee, preaching [the gospel] in their synagogues and casting out demons.
AMP.40: And a leper came to Him, begging Him and falling on his knees before Him, saying, "If You are willing, You are able to make me clean."
AMP.41: Moved with compassion [for his suffering], Jesus reached out with His hand and touched him, and said to him, "I am willing; be cleansed."
AMP.42: The leprosy left him immediately and he was cleansed [completely healed and restored to health].
AMP.43: And Jesus [deeply moved] admonished him sternly and sent him away immediately,
AMP.44: saying to him, "See that you tell no one anything [about this]; but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your purification what Moses commanded, as proof to them [that you are really healed]."
AMP.45: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely and to spread the news [of his healing], to such an extent that Jesus could no longer openly enter a city [where He was known], but stayed out in the unpopulated places; yet people were still coming to Him from everywhere.

MSG.1: The good news of Jesus Christ—the Message!—begins here,
MSG.2: following to the letter the scroll of the prophet Isaiah. Watch closely: I'm sending my preacher ahead of you; He'll make the road smooth for you.
MSG.3: Thunder in the desert! Prepare for God's arrival! Make the road smooth and straight!
MSG.4: John the Baptizer appeared in the wild, preaching a baptism of life-change that leads to forgiveness of sins.
MSG.5: People thronged to him from Judea and Jerusalem and, as they confessed their sins, were baptized by him in the Jordan River into a changed life.
MSG.6: John wore a camel-hair habit, tied at the waist with a leather belt. He ate locusts and wild field honey.
MSG.7: As he preached he said, "The real action comes next: The star in this drama, to whom I'm a mere stagehand, will change your life.
MSG.8: I'm baptizing you here in the river, turning your old life in for a kingdom life. His baptism—a holy baptism by the Holy Spirit—will change you from the inside out."
MSG.9: At this time, Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
MSG.10: The moment he came out of the water, he saw the sky split open and God's Spirit, looking like a dove, come down on him.
MSG.11: Along with the Spirit, a voice: "You are my Son, chosen and marked by my love, pride of my life."
MSG.12: At once, this same Spirit pushed Jesus out into the wild.
MSG.13: For forty wilderness days and nights he was tested by Satan. Wild animals were his companions, and angels took care of him.
MSG.14: After John was arrested, Jesus went to Galilee preaching the Message of God:
MSG.15: "Time's up! God's kingdom is here. Change your life and believe the Message."
MSG.16: Passing along the beach of Lake Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew net-fishing. Fishing was their regular work.
MSG.17: Jesus said to them, "Come with me. I'll make a new kind of fisherman out of you. I'll show you how to catch men and women instead of perch and bass."
MSG.18: They didn't ask questions. They dropped their nets and followed.
MSG.19: A dozen yards or so down the beach, he saw the brothers James and John, Zebedee's sons. They were in the boat, mending their fishnets.
MSG.20: Right off, he made the same offer. Immediately, they left their father Zebedee, the boat, and the hired hands, and followed.
MSG.21: Then they entered Capernaum. When the Sabbath arrived, Jesus lost no time in getting to the meeting place. He spent the day there teaching.
MSG.22: They were surprised at his teaching—so forthright, so confident—not quibbling and quoting like the religion scholars.
MSG.23: Suddenly, while still in the meeting place, he was interrupted by a man who was deeply disturbed and yelling out,
MSG.24: "What business do you have here with us, Jesus? Nazarene! I know what you're up to! You're the Holy One of God, and you've come to destroy us!"
MSG.25: Jesus shut him up: "Quiet! Get out of him!"
MSG.26: The afflicting spirit threw the man into spasms, protesting loudly—and got out.
MSG.27: Everyone there was incredulous, buzzing with curiosity. "What's going on here? A new teaching that does what it says? He shuts up defiling, demonic spirits and sends them packing!"
MSG.28: News of this traveled fast and was soon all over Galilee.
MSG.29: Directly on leaving the meeting place, they came to Simon and Andrew's house, accompanied by James and John.
MSG.30: Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed, burning up with fever. They told Jesus.
MSG.31: He went to her, took her hand, and raised her up. No sooner had the fever left than she was up fixing dinner for them.
MSG.32: That evening, after the sun was down, they brought sick and evil-afflicted people to him,
MSG.33: the whole city lined up at his door!
MSG.34: He cured their sick bodies and tormented spirits. Because the demons knew his true identity, he didn't let them say a word.
MSG.35: While it was still night, way before dawn, he got up and went out to a secluded spot and prayed.
MSG.36: Simon and those with him went looking for him.
MSG.37: They found him and said, "Everybody's looking for you."
MSG.38: Jesus said, "Let's go to the rest of the villages so I can preach there also. This is why I've come."
MSG.39: He went to their meeting places all through Galilee, preaching and throwing out the demons.
MSG.40: A leper came to him, begging on his knees, "If you want to, you can cleanse me."
MSG.41: Deeply moved, Jesus put out his hand, touched him, and said, "I want to. Be clean."
MSG.42: Then and there the leprosy was gone, his skin smooth and healthy.
MSG.43: Jesus dismissed him with strict orders:
MSG.44: "Say nothing to anyone. Take the offering for cleansing that Moses prescribed and present yourself to the priest. This will validate your healing to the people."
MSG.45: But as soon as the man was out of earshot, he told everyone he met what had happened, spreading the news all over town. So Jesus kept to out-of-the-way places, no longer able to move freely in and out of the city. But people found him, and came from all over.

NIV.1:
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
The beginning of the gospel about Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
NIV.2:
It is written in Isaiah the prophet:
“I will send my messenger ahead of you,
who will prepare your way”—
NIV.3:
“a voice of one calling in the desert,
‘Prepare the way for the Lord,
make straight paths for him.’”

NIV.6: John wore clothing made of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
NIV.7: And this was his message: “After me will come one more powerful than I, the thongs of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.

The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
At that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
NIV.10: As Jesus was coming up out of the water, he saw heaven being torn open and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
NIV.11: And a voice came from heaven: “You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased.”
NIV.12: At once the Spirit sent him out into the desert,
NIV.13: and he was in the desert forty days, being tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals, and angels attended him.
NIV.14:
The Calling of the First Disciples
After John was put in prison, Jesus went into Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God.
NIV.15: “The time has come,” he said. “The kingdom of God is near. Repent and believe the good news!”
NIV.16: As Jesus walked beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen.
NIV.17: “Come, follow me,” Jesus said, “and I will make you fishers of men.”
NIV.18: At once they left their nets and followed him.
NIV.19: When he had gone a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John in a boat, preparing their nets.
NIV.20: Without delay he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men and followed him.
NIV.21:
Jesus Drives Out an Evil Spirit
They went to Capernaum, and when the Sabbath came, Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.
NIV.22: The people were amazed at his teaching, because he taught them as one who had authority, not as the teachers of the law.
NIV.23: Just then a man in their synagogue who was possessed by an evil spirit cried out,
NIV.24: “What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
NIV.25: “Be quiet!” said Jesus sternly. “Come out of him!”
NIV.26: The evil spirit shook the man violently and came out of him with a shriek.
NIV.27: The people were all so amazed that they asked each other, “What is this? A new teaching—and with authority! He even gives orders to evil spirits and they obey him.”
NIV.28: News about him spread quickly over the whole region of Galilee.
NIV.29:
Jesus Heals Many
As soon as they left the synagogue, they went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.
NIV.30: Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told Jesus about her.
NIV.31: So he went to her, took her hand and helped her up. The fever left her and she began to wait on them.
NIV.32: That evening after sunset the people brought to Jesus all the sick and demon-possessed.
NIV.33: The whole town gathered at the door,
NIV.34: and Jesus healed many who had various diseases. He also drove out many demons, but he would not let the demons speak because they knew who he was.
NIV.35:
Jesus Prays in a Solitary Place
Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house and went off to a solitary place, where he prayed.
NIV.36: Simon and his companions went to look for him,
NIV.37: and when they found him, they exclaimed: “Everyone is looking for you!”
NIV.38: Jesus replied, “Let us go somewhere else—to the nearby villages—so I can preach there also. That is why I have come.”
NIV.39: So he traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.
NIV.40:
A Man With Leprosy
A man with leprosy came to him and begged him on his knees, “If you are willing, you can make me clean.”
NIV.41: Filled with compassion, Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” he said. “Be clean!”
NIV.42: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cured.
NIV.43: Jesus sent him away at once with a strong warning:
NIV.44: “See that you don’t tell this to anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.”
NIV.45: Instead he went out and began to talk freely, spreading the news. As a result, Jesus could no longer enter a town openly but stayed outside in lonely places. Yet the people still came to him from everywhere.
